title: Run `podman machine init` as root, and /etc/containers/containers.conf is created with permissions of 600.
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

First, when I run `podman machine init` as root, `/etc/containers/containers.conf` is created with permission of 600.
Second, when I run any `podman` command as non-root, I get a permission denied error.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Run `podman machine init` as root.

```
# podman machine init
Extracting compressed file
# ls -l /etc/containers/containers.conf
-rw-------. 1 root root 602  6月 20 03:25 /etc/containers/containers.conf
```

2. Run any `podman` command as non-root, I get a permission denied error.

```
$ podman version
ERRO[0000] reading system config "/etc/containers/containers.conf": decode configuration /etc/containers/containers.conf: open /etc/containers/containers.conf: permission denied
```

**Describe the results you received:**

```
$ podman version
ERRO[0000] reading system config "/etc/containers/containers.conf": decode configuration /etc/containers/containers.conf: open /etc/containers/containers.conf: permission denied
```

**Describe the results you expected:**

`/etc/containers/containers.conf` will be created with the correct permissions. Any `podman` command can be executed by a non-root user.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:      3.2.1
API Version:  3.2.1
Go Version:   go1.16.3
Built:        Tue Jun 15 04:12:29 2021
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md)**

Yes

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

- Fedora release 34 (Thirty Four)
- VM on VMware ESXi

comments.body:
Thanks for opening the issue, @tnk4on!

@ashley-cui, can you have a look?  Why is Podman creating containers.conf?
Huh interesting bug. I'm able to reproduce, will dig into it now!
We should not create that file unless content is being added, it and should be 644, if created.
This has been fixed in master and is backported via https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/10763 to the soon to be released v3.2.2.
I confirmed the fix in v3.2.2. Thank you!
